summary: Installed project dependencies via pip install -e '.[dev]' in the existing .venv. All 118 tests across 7 test files now pass. The loguru module (and all other deps) were already specified in pyproject.toml but had not been installed in the venv. No source code or test files were modified.

acceptance_results.criterion: All Python dependencies from pyproject.toml are installed in the venv
acceptance_results.evidence: pip install -e '.[dev]' completed successfully — loguru, httpx, beautifulsoup4, psycopg, pydantic, click, openpyxl, tenacity, fastapi, uvicorn, pytest, pytest-cov, ruff, mypy all installed

acceptance_results.criterion: pytest runs all 5 test files without collection errors
acceptance_results.evidence: pytest collected 118 items across 7 test files (test_models, test_normalizers, test_parser, test_twogis, test_website_contacts, test_yandex_maps, test_zakupki) — zero collection errors
118 passed in 1.46s — zero failures, zero errors, zero skipped

acceptance_results.criterion: No new dependencies added beyond what pyproject.toml already specifies
acceptance_results.evidence: Only ran pip install on existing pyproject.toml — no modifications to dependency list

risks: The .venv uses Python 3.14 which is newer than the pyproject.toml minimum of 3.11 — no issues observed but worth noting
follow_up_tasks: Add .venv setup step to QUICKSTART.md documentation
Consider adding a Makefile target for test setup
